chapter_title.en: Chapter 33 – Waiting period of the woman with irregular menstruations

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/170)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/170)
text: امْرَأَةٍ طُلِّقَتْ وَ قَدْ طَعَنَتْ فِي السِّنِّ فَحَاضَتْ حَيْضَةً وَاحِدَةً ثُمَّ ارْتَفَعَ حَيْضُهَا فَقَالَ تَعْتَدُّ بِالْحَيْضَةِ وَ شَهْرَيْنِ مُسْتَقْبِلَيْنِ فَإِنَّهَا قَدْ يَئِسَتْ مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Yazeed Bin Is’haq Shaeer, from Haroun Bin Hamza,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a woman who is divorced, and she is advanced in the age. So she menstruated with one menstruation, then her menstruations were lifted. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘She should wait with the menstruation and two months, consecutively, so she is one who has despaired from the menstruation (reached menopause)’.
en.sarwar: 11. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Yazid ibn Ishaq Sha‘ir from Harun ibn Hamzah who has said the following: “About the case of a woman who is divorced and is old but experiences Hayd (menses) once then her Hayd stops, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘She must count her waiting period for one Hayd and two months in future because she has reached menopause.’”
